questions.question: Computer programs are written using special languages called:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Computer programs are written using special languages called programming languages.

questions.question: There are several programming languages. Each has its own:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Each programming language has its own grammar called its syntax.

questions.question: Machine level and Assembly language are examples of:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Machine level and assembly language are examples of low-level languages.

questions.question: A special program called Assembler converts all instructions into:
questions.explanation: Page 493: A special program called Assembler converts all instructions into the binary format.

questions.question: Because all such instructions must finally be converted to binary form, all high-level languages have their own translator programs called:
questions.explanation: Page 493: All high-level languages have their own form of translator—programs called compilers or interpreters.

questions.question: Software relates to set of programs. The software that makes the computer hardware parts operational is called:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Software relates to set of programs. Software that makes hardware parts operational is called operational/system software. In other words, it governs the operations of a computer system.

questions.question: Examples of popular high-level languages are:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Examples of popular high-level languages are C, C++, Java, FORTRAN, Pascal.

questions.question: Operating systems like Unix, MS-DOS, Linux, Windows, Mac OS are some of:

options.B: The most popular operating systems used by computers

questions.explanation: Page 493: Unix, MS-DOS, Linux, Windows, Mac OS are some of the most popular operating systems.

questions.question: The most important system's program is:
questions.explanation: Page 493: The most important system's program is the operating system.

questions.question: Another popular type of application programs is the Database Management Systems (DBMS). A common term used for application programs that permit text editing is:
questions.explanation: Page 493: A common term for application programs for text editing is word processors.

questions.question: Software is generally divided into two kinds:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Software generally divided into two kinds of programs: system software (collectively called the hardware) and application programs.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + A is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + A = Select All.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + B is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + B = Bold.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + C is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + C = Copy.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + D is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + D = Font Dialogue Box.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + E is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + E = Centre Alignment.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + I is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + I = Italics.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + J is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + J = Justified Alignment.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + N is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + N = Opens New blank document.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + O is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + O = Opens existing document.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + R is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + R = Right Alignment.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Esc is used for:

options.B: Displays the Start Menu in the Windows OS

questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + Esc = Displays the Start Menu in the Windows OS.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut PrtScn is used for:

options.B: Print screen / Take a screen shot

questions.explanation: Page 493: PrtScn = Print screen / Take a screen shot.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Alt + Tab is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Alt + Tab = Switch to next opened program.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + End is used to:

options.B: Go to end of a File/Worksheet

questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + End = Go to end of a File/Worksheet.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Home is used to:

options.B: Go to beginning of a File/Worksheet

questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + Home = Go to beginning of a File/Worksheet.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Alt + Shift + Tab is used to:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Alt + Shift + Tab = Switch to previous opened Program.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Alt + F4 is used to:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Alt + F4 = Close windows/Shut down.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Z is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + Z = Undo.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Y is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + Y = Redo.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + W is used to:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + W = Close File.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + P is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + P = Print.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + U is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + U = Underline.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + K is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + K = Hyperlink.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + S is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Ctrl + S = Save.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut F7 is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: F7 = Spell Check.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut F2 is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: F2 = Edit/Rename.

questions.question: The keyboard shortcut F5 is used for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: F5 = Go to.

questions.question: ALU stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: ALU: It stands for Arithmetic Logic Unit. All calculations in computer are done here.

questions.question: BIOS stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: BIOS: It stands for Basic Input Output System.

questions.question: Boot Loader reads the main portion of the operating system from:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Boot Loader: It reads the main portion of the operating system from secondary memory.

questions.question: Bit is the short form of:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Bit: It is the short form of Binary Digit.

questions.question: A program stored in ROM is called:
questions.explanation: Page 493: BIOS/program is stored in ROM.

questions.question: Batch Processing means:

options.B: Data are processed in a batch

questions.explanation: Page 493: Batch Processing: Data are processed in a batch.

questions.question: CU stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 493: CU: It stands for Control Unit. It controls the computer system.

questions.question: Browsing means:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Browsing: Viewing webpages on the website.

questions.question: Byte is defined as:

options.B: One byte is a collection of 8 bits

questions.explanation: Page 493: Byte: One byte is a collection of 8 bits.

questions.question: Cell in a spreadsheet refers to:

options.B: Boxes created by the intersection of rows and columns

questions.explanation: Page 493: Cell: Cells are boxes created by the intersection of rows and columns.

questions.question: A Cracker is a person who breaks into a:

options.B: Computer system to steal information as programs for unauthorized use

questions.explanation: Page 493: Cracker: A cracker is a person who breaks into a computer system to steal information as programs for unauthorized use.

questions.question: Copyright means the material and information are the property of:
questions.explanation: Page 493: Copyright: It means the material and information are the personal property of the owner or producer.
